info.description: OCPCTL is a self-service OpenShift cluster provisioning and management platform. This API provides endpoints for creating, managing, and destroying OpenShift clusters with automated lifecycle management, policy enforcement, and resource tracking.
info.title: OCPCTL API

get.description: Returns aggregated statistics for all clusters (admin only)
Lists orphaned AWS resources that were detected by the janitor. Supports filtering by status, type, and region.

parameters.description: Filter by resource type (VPC, LoadBalancer, HostedZone, DNSRecord, EC2Instance, S3Bucket)
parameters.name: type

get.description: Returns aggregated statistics about orphaned resources grouped by type and status

delete.description: Actually deletes the orphaned resource from AWS (currently supports HostedZone and DNSRecord only). Other resource types must be deleted manually in AWS Console.

400.description: Resource type not supported for automated deletion

500.description: Failed to delete resource from AWS

patch.description: Marks an orphaned resource as ignored (e.g., false positive or intentionally kept)
Marks an orphaned resource as resolved (e.g., after manual cleanup in AWS Console)

get.description: Returns information about API server, workers, and autoscaling groups
Returns all API keys for the authenticated user. Plaintext keys are never returned.

post.description: Generates a new API key for the authenticated user. The plaintext key is only returned once on creation.

delete.description: Permanently deletes an API key. Only the key owner can delete it.

patch.description: Updates the name of an API key. Only the key owner can update it.

post.description: Revokes an API key, preventing further use. Only the key owner can revoke it.
Authenticates user with email and password. Returns JWT access token and sets httpOnly refresh token cookie.
Revokes refresh token and clears authentication cookies

get.description: Returns profile information for the authenticated user

patch.description: Updates profile information for the authenticated user (username, timezone, work hours)

post.description: Changes the authenticated user's password. Requires current password. Invalidates all sessions.
Generates a new access token using the refresh token from httpOnly cookie

get.description: Lists all clusters accessible to the authenticated user. Admins see all clusters, regular users see only their own.

post.description: Creates a new OpenShift cluster with the specified configuration. Validates against policy engine and initiates async provisioning. Supports post-deployment configuration via pre-approved add-ons (with version selection) or custom operators/manifests.

get.description: Retrieves details of a specific cluster by ID

delete.description: Initiates cluster destruction. Creates a background job to deprovision all cluster resources.

get.description: Returns all post-deployment configurations for a cluster

patch.description: Retries a failed post-deployment configuration by creating a new job

400.description: Configuration not failed or doesn't belong to cluster

post.description: Manually triggers post-deployment configuration for a ready cluster (useful if skipped during creation)

400.description: Cluster not ready, already configured, or job already running

patch.description: Extends the time-to-live (TTL) of a cluster, postponing its automatic destruction

post.description: Hibernates a cluster by stopping its instances. Reduces costs during off-hours. (AWS only)

400.description: Cluster not ready or platform not supported

get.description: Downloads the kubeconfig file for a ready cluster as a YAML attachment
Returns a pre-signed S3 URL for downloading the kubeconfig (15-minute expiration)

400.description: Cluster not ready or S3 storage not configured

get.description: Returns deployment logs for a cluster with cursor-based pagination. Returns logs from all jobs (CREATE, POST_CONFIGURE, etc.) if job_id not specified.

parameters.description: Job ID to get logs for (returns logs from all jobs if not specified)
parameters.name: job_id

parameters.description: Number of log lines to return (default 500)
parameters.name: limit

get.description: Returns cluster outputs including kubeconfig, credentials, API URLs, and console URL. Only available for ready clusters.

400.description: Cluster not ready or outputs not available

post.description: Extracts cluster outputs from the install directory and updates the database. Useful if outputs become stale.
Resumes a hibernated cluster by starting its instances. (AWS only)

400.description: Cluster not hibernating or platform not supported

get.description: Returns all storage groups linked to this cluster

post.description: Links persistent storage from another cluster to this cluster

delete.description: Removes the storage group link from this cluster

404.description: Cluster or storage link not found

get.description: Returns basic health status of the API server including auth availability
Returns a paginated list of jobs. Can be filtered by cluster_id, type (create/destroy), and status.

parameters.description: Filter by status (pending, running, completed, failed)
parameters.name: status

get.description: Retrieves details of a specific job by ID
Lists all enabled post-config add-ons with version information. Supports filtering by category, platform, and search query. Each add-on includes multiple versions with one marked as default.

parameters.description: Filter by category (backup, migration, cicd, monitoring, security, storage, networking)
parameters.name: category

parameters.description: Filter by supported platform (openshift, eks, iks)
parameters.name: platform

post.description: Validates custom post-deployment configuration without creating a cluster. Checks resource limits, dependencies, conditions, and generates execution DAG.

get.description: Returns all available cluster profiles. Can be filtered by platform (aws or ibmcloud).
Returns details of a specific cluster profile by name
Checks if the server is ready to handle requests by verifying database connectivity
Lists post-configuration templates visible to the authenticated user

post.description: Creates a new post-configuration template for reuse

get.description: Retrieves a specific template by ID

delete.description: Deletes a template (only owner or admin can delete)

patch.description: Updates a template (only owner can update)

get.description: Returns a paginated list of users (admin only). Password hashes are excluded from response. Supports pagination via query parameters.

parameters.description: Maximum number of users to return (default: 50, max: 100)
parameters.name: limit

200.description: Returns users array, total count, limit, and offset

post.description: Creates a new user with specified email, username, role, and password (admin only)

get.description: Retrieves user details by ID (admin only)

delete.description: Soft deletes a user by marking as inactive (admin only). Also revokes all refresh tokens.

400.description: User ID required or cannot delete self

patch.description: Updates user details including username, role, timezone, work hours, or active status (admin only)

get.description: Returns version, commit hash, and build time of the API server

ami_family.description: For managed node groups (AmazonLinux2023, AmazonLinux2, etc.)

source.description: e.g. "redhat-operators" (optional - OLM will search all catalogs if omitted)

plain_key.description: Full key, only shown on creation

values.description: Helm values (supports {{.Variable}} templating in string values)

isPublic.description: If true, visible to all users

description.example: Backup and restore OpenShift clusters and applications

customPostConfig.description: Custom post-deployment operators, scripts, and manifests

BearerAuth.description: JWT access token. Format: "Bearer {token}". Obtain from /api/v1/auth/login endpoint.
BearerAuth.name: Authorization
